summary_one_line: Profile color-space signature at offset 0x10-0x13 must be a FOURCC outside the recognized cmsSig* cases to reach the switch default arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte color-space signature read into `Space` (input offset 0x10-0x13) must NOT equal any of the recognized switch cases (cmsSigGrayData, cmsSigRgbData='RGB ', cmsSigLabData, cmsSigCmykData, cmsSigCmyData); only a non-matching signature falls through to the `default:;` arm at line 752.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry non-standard FOURCCs at offset 0x10-0x13 — e.g. 4d 43 48 35 ('MCH5'), 4d 43 48 31 ('MCH1'), 4d 59 20 ('MY '), which match no switch case, so `switch (Space)` falls through to the blocked default arm; the per-role overlay shows line 752 hit only by W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry the literal 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offset 0x10-0x13, which dispatches to `case cmsSigRgbData` (line 732, hit by both W and L) and returns TRUE before ever reaching `default:;`, so the loser is permanently stuck on the false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback: the equality comparisons that implement the switch on `Space` are logged by CmpLogObserver, and I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand bytes into the input position holding the color-space signature, flipping it away from 'RGB ' to a non-recognized value that takes the default arm. Plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it can only nudge the FOURCC via Hamming-bucket gradient and never escapes the 'RGB ' attractor in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on the color-space Space, reachable only when Space matches no enumerated case.
Line 752 (the default arm) is hit only by the winner-resolving seed set.
Winner seeds carry non-standard FOURCC color-space signatures at offset 0x10 such as 'MCH5'.
Loser seeds carry the literal 'RGB ' signature at offset 0x10, which matches a switch case.
The byte diff shows offset 0x10-0x12 differs: winners vary while every loser holds R/G/B.
In the loser, the RGB case arm (line 732) is taken, diverting flow before the default arm.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace while value_profile_cmplog has it.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2242 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0cae0d2f5478842f` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 15 of the 16-level ancestor chain, the characteristic ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch operands (the cmsSig* FOURCC constants) are NOT input-byte pre-images — e.g. derived at runtime — yet value_profile_cmplog still resolves the default arm at the same rate as plain value_profile, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of the color-space signature.

weakest_evidence_point: The default arm could also be reachable by inputs whose Space byte is corrupted/zeroed (seed 1208988c94dacf3f shows 'MY ' with leading zeros), so I cannot fully exclude that some winner resolutions came from generic havoc truncation rather than a targeted I2S FOURCC substitution.
